chunks.text: Dogs derive from wolves some fifteen thousand years ago.
Through living with humans, they acquired roles as hunters, guards, and companions.
In modern times, dogs play various role.
Guide dogs, police dogs, therapy dogs.
They are intelligent, sensitive, and perceive human emotions with surprising accuracy.
Scientists have demonstrated that dogs read human facial expressions and show distinct emotional responses.
When an owner is sad, the dog seems sad.
When an owner is happy, the dog wags its tail.
Pet therapy, which eases the loneliness of the elderly
has also been gaining attention in recent years.
For an elderly person living alone
a dog becomes a member of the family and a daily source of meaning.
But keeping a dog also comes with responsibility.
Food, medical costs, time for walks.
One must be prepared for a commitment of fifteen years or more.
Sadly, there are owners who line up excuse and abandon their animals.
Not a few dogs are sent to shelters and meet an unfortunate fate.
To take an animal as family is to carry responsibility to the end.
Those who cannot should not have the thought to dispose of them.
The bond between dogs and humans is a special one, nurtured over thousands of years.
Without disturb to this bond, we should keep caring for it.
